title: Ο περί Επεξεργασίας Δεδομένων Προσωπικού Χαρακτήρα (Προστασία του Ατόμου) Νόμος (Τηλεπικοινωνίες) (Νόμος 92(Ι)/2007)
title_en: Processing of Personal Data (Telecommunications Sector) Law (Law 92(I)/2007)
description: This Law transposes the EU ePrivacy Directive (2002/58/EC) into Cypriot law and governs the processing of personal data in the electronic communications sector. It establishes obligations for providers of publicly available electronic communications services regarding confidentiality, security, traffic data, location data, and unsolicited communications. The Law complements the general personal data protection framework by addressing sector-specific requirements for telecommunications.

provisions.title: Article 1. Short Title
provisions.content: This Law may be cited as the Processing of Personal Data (Telecommunications Sector) Law of 2007. It constitutes the primary legal instrument governing the collection, processing, storage, and use of personal data by providers of publicly available electronic communications services and networks in the Republic of Cyprus. The Law applies to all entities operating within the electronic communications sector that process subscriber or user data in the course of providing services to the public, and establishes a framework harmonised with European Union requirements under Directive 2002/58/EC.

provisions.title: Article 2. Scope of Application
provisions.content: The provisions of this Law apply to the processing of personal data in connection with the provision of publicly available electronic communications services in public communications networks in the Republic of Cyprus. The Law covers all categories of data processed in the course of providing such services, including traffic data, location data, subscriber data, and data relating to communications content where applicable. Providers of electronic communications services operating across multiple jurisdictions shall comply with this Law in respect of services directed at or provided from within the territory of Cyprus. The Law does not apply to activities outside the scope of Union law or to activities of public authorities in relation to criminal investigations.

provisions.title: Article 3. Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Law, 'electronic communications service' means a service normally provided for remuneration via electronic communications networks, as defined under the Electronic Communications Law. 'Traffic data' means any data processed for the purpose of conveying a communication on an electronic communications network or for the billing thereof. 'Location data' means any data processed in an electronic communications network, indicating the geographic position of the terminal equipment of a user of a publicly available electronic communications service. 'Subscriber' means any natural or legal person who or which is party to a contract with the provider of publicly available electronic communications services for the supply of such services. 'User' means any natural person using a publicly available electronic communications service.

provisions.title: Article 4. Confidentiality of Communications
provisions.content: Providers of publicly available electronic communications services and networks shall ensure the confidentiality of communications and the related traffic data by means of national legislation. In particular, providers shall prohibit listening, tapping, storage or other kinds of interception or surveillance of communications and related traffic data by persons other than users, without the consent of the users concerned, except where legally authorised to do so in accordance with applicable law. This obligation applies to all technical staff employed by providers and to persons acting under the direction of providers. The confidentiality obligation extends to content as well as metadata associated with communications, and encompasses both real-time communications and stored communications data.

provisions.title: Article 5. Security of Processing
provisions.content: Providers of publicly available electronic communications services shall take appropriate technical and organisational measures to safeguard the security of their services, if necessary in conjunction with the provider of the public communications network with respect to network security. Having regard to the state of the art and the cost of their implementation, these measures shall ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk presented. The competent authority, which is the Commissioner for Personal Data Protection, may audit compliance with security obligations and may require providers to submit information demonstrating that appropriate measures are in place. Where a particular risk of a breach of the security of the network exists, the provider shall inform subscribers concerning such risk and any possible remedies.

provisions.title: Article 6. Traffic Data
provisions.content: Traffic data relating to subscribers and users processed and stored by providers of publicly available electronic communications services or networks must be erased or made anonymous when no longer needed for the purpose of the transmission of a communication. Traffic data necessary for the purposes of subscriber billing and interconnection payments may be processed only up to the end of the period during which the bill may lawfully be challenged or payment pursued. Providers may process traffic data for the purpose of marketing electronic communications services or for the provision of value added services to the extent and for the duration necessary for such services or marketing, if the subscriber or user to whom the data relates has given prior consent. Providers shall inform subscribers or users of the types of traffic data which are processed and of the duration of such processing.

provisions.title: Article 7. Location Data Other Than Traffic Data
provisions.content: Where location data other than traffic data relating to users or subscribers of public electronic communications networks or publicly available electronic communications services can be processed, such data may only be processed when they are made anonymous, or with the prior consent of the users or subscribers to the extent and for the duration necessary for the provision of a value added service. The provider shall inform users and subscribers of the type of location data other than traffic data which will be processed, of the purposes and duration of the processing, and whether the data will be transmitted to a third party for the purpose of providing the value added service. Users and subscribers shall be given the possibility to withdraw their consent for the processing of location data at any time.

provisions.title: Article 8. Itemised Billing
provisions.content: Subscribers shall have the right to receive non-itemised bills. Where itemised bills are provided, the basic level of itemisation shall be determined by the competent regulatory authority and shall be offered free of charge to allow subscribers to verify the charges. The Commissioner for Personal Data Protection shall have regard to the need to reconcile the rights of subscribers receiving itemised bills with the privacy rights of calling users and called subscribers, in particular with regard to calling line identification. Advanced or enhanced itemisation shall be provided upon request where technically feasible, subject to any privacy-protective measures required by the competent authority.

provisions.title: Article 9. Calling and Connected Line Identification
provisions.content: Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, the provider of publicly available electronic communications services shall offer the calling user the possibility of preventing the presentation of the calling line identification on a per-call basis. Calling line identification shall also be preventable on a per-line basis. Where presentation of calling line identification is offered, providers shall offer the called subscriber the possibility of rejecting incoming calls where the calling line identification has been prevented by the calling user. The connected line identification presented to the calling user shall be preventable by the called subscriber. All these possibilities shall be offered free of charge to users and subscribers.

provisions.title: Article 10. Automatic Call Forwarding
provisions.content: Providers of publicly available electronic communications services shall implement measures to ensure that subscribers can terminate automatic call forwarding by a third party to the subscriber's terminal without charge. This provision applies where a third party has established an automatic forwarding arrangement that redirects calls from the subscriber's terminal to another destination, whether within the Republic of Cyprus or to a destination outside its territory. Providers shall maintain the technical capability to honour subscriber requests for termination of such forwarding arrangements within a reasonable timeframe, and shall not impose additional charges upon subscribers exercising this right. The Commissioner may specify technical standards for the implementation of this obligation.

provisions.title: Article 11. Directories of Subscribers
provisions.content: Personal data contained in publicly available directories of subscribers of electronic communications services shall be limited to what is necessary to identify a particular subscriber and shall not be used for purposes other than that for which it was collected. Providers shall ensure that subscribers are informed, free of charge and before being included in the directory, about the purpose or purposes of a printed or electronic directory of subscribers in which their personal data can be included, and about any further usage possibilities based on search functions embedded in electronic versions of the directory. Subscribers shall be given the opportunity to determine whether their personal data are included in a public directory, and if so which data, and to verify, correct or withdraw such data at any time without charge.

provisions.title: Article 12. Unsolicited Communications
provisions.content: The use of automated calling and communication systems without human intervention, facsimile machines, or electronic mail for the purposes of direct marketing may only be allowed in respect of subscribers who have given their prior consent. Notwithstanding the foregoing, where a natural or legal person obtains electronic contact details from its customers in the context of the sale of a product or service, the same natural or legal person may use these electronic contact details for direct marketing of its own similar products or services provided that customers clearly and distinctly are given the opportunity to object, free of charge and in an easy manner, to such use at the time of collection and on the occasion of each message. The practice of sending electronic mail for purposes of direct marketing disguising or concealing the identity of the sender is prohibited.

provisions.title: Article 13. Technical Features and Standardisation
provisions.content: Where required, providers of publicly available electronic communications services shall take the necessary measures to inform subscribers of the technical features of their networks and services relevant to privacy and personal data protection. The Commissioner for Personal Data Protection, in coordination with the Office of the Commissioner of Electronic Communications and Postal Regulation, may issue guidance on technical standards applicable to electronic communications providers for the implementation of privacy-protective measures. Standards adopted at European Union level for the purposes of ensuring the consistent application of privacy requirements across the Union shall be taken into account when specifying national technical requirements.

provisions.title: Article 14. Enforcement and Supervision
provisions.content: The Commissioner for Personal Data Protection is designated as the supervisory authority responsible for monitoring and enforcing compliance with the provisions of this Law. The Commissioner has the power to investigate complaints submitted by subscribers or users, to conduct audits of providers, to issue orders requiring providers to bring processing operations into compliance, and to impose administrative sanctions in accordance with the general data protection framework. The Commissioner shall cooperate with the Office of the Commissioner of Electronic Communications and Postal Regulation in matters falling within the competence of both authorities, and may jointly conduct inspections where circumstances require coordination between the regulatory bodies.

provisions.title: Article 15. Exemptions for Law Enforcement Purposes
provisions.content: Member States may adopt legislative measures to restrict the scope of the rights and obligations provided for in this Law when such restriction constitutes a necessary, appropriate and proportionate measure within a democratic society to safeguard national security, defence, public security, and the prevention, investigation, detection and prosecution of criminal offences or of unauthorised use of the electronic communication system. To this end, providers of publicly available electronic communications services may retain data specified in orders issued under applicable legislation, subject to the requirements of proportionality, necessity, and compliance with fundamental rights as guaranteed by the Constitution of the Republic and applicable Union law.

provisions.title: Article 16. Remedies and Liability
provisions.content: Any person who suffers damage as a result of an unlawful processing operation or of any action incompatible with the provisions of this Law is entitled to receive compensation from the provider for the damage suffered. Providers may be exempted from this liability if they prove that they are not responsible for the event giving rise to the damage. Subscribers and users may lodge complaints with the Commissioner for Personal Data Protection, whose decisions may be appealed before the competent courts. The availability of administrative remedies does not preclude recourse to judicial proceedings, and the courts of the Republic of Cyprus shall have jurisdiction to hear claims arising under this Law.

definitions.definition: A service normally provided for remuneration via electronic communications networks, not including services providing or exercising editorial control over content transmitted using electronic communications networks and services.
Any data processed for the purpose of conveying a communication on an electronic communications network or for the billing thereof.
Any data processed in an electronic communications network, indicating the geographic position of the terminal equipment of a user of a publicly available electronic communications service.
Any natural or legal person who or which is party to a contract with the provider of publicly available electronic communications services for the supply of such services.
Any natural person using a publicly available electronic communications service, for private or business purposes, without necessarily being subscribed to this service.
